verdicts.notes: The short fruit noun makes an appropriate guided H1 exercise. Alternate wording and a lowercased misspelling both match, while spacing, ATX form, and H1 presence each have direct failing evidence.
The familiar reading phrase stays within Level 1 typing load and teaches one ATX H1. The independent prose and spelling variants pass, and all three declared structural checks are exercised directly.

The everyday weather phrase is a clear guided production target. Real-engine fixtures prove wording and case tolerance, the requested hash syntax, malformed spacing feedback, and the optional multiple-title review.
The learning-tool vocabulary and visible pattern fit Level 1. The match path depends only on heading spacing, ATX source form, and H1 presence; different prose and spelling variation remain Matched.

The daily-travel phrase is suitable for hidden-syntax H1 recall. The transcript confirms structure-only acceptance across alternate prose and spelling variation, plus direct evidence for each heading check.

The errands vocabulary keeps recall approachable without supplying semantic grading. Alternate text and the case-spelling fixture pass, while spacing, style, and missing-H1 cases fail with their declared IDs.

The routine phrase is a low-friction Level 2 recall prompt. Verification shows no title-text dependency and binds all ATX H1 checks, including the expected optional review for a second document title.

The familiar planning phrase fits recall and differs from the other variants. Independent prose and misspelling evidence match, and every declared heading predicate has a direct real-engine failure fixture.

The customer-note anatomy checks one H1-led outline, two H2 sections, strong emphasis in the first section, and a three-item unordered follow-up list. All five checks have direct fixtures and alternate workplace prose remains Matched.

The decision-record structure is readable and level-appropriate: title, two ordered sections, highlighted decision, then action list. Prose variation is accepted and outline, hierarchy, count, emphasis, and list-shape feedback are directly verified.

The onboarding guide uses the same inspectable Level 3 anatomy with distinct workplace content. The real engine accepts unrelated prose and spelling variation and reports each structural omission under its expected check ID.

The handoff target teaches a compact human-scannable title, update section, emphasized status, and follow-up list. Each declared predicate is covered directly without checking owners, wording, spelling, or semantic correctness.

The accessibility spec requires a stable five-section outline, ordered implementation, unordered acceptance list, and one fenced language-tagged verification block. All six checks are directly exercised and different technical prose still matches.

The notification-retry target is a distinct executable-spec scenario with the declared five-section/list/code anatomy. The transcript proves direct coverage, malformed-fence feedback, alternate prose tolerance, and review-only treatment of an extra H1.

The search-filter spec has a clear Level 4 scope-to-verification flow. Structural checks cover the exact section count and sequence, hierarchy, ordered work, acceptance list, and fenced command block without validating technical claims.

The bug-fix scenario gives a concise executable-spec composition with dependencies, ordered work, acceptance items, and verification code. All declared checks have isolated failures, while altered prose and spelling remain passing.

Revision 2 preserves a strong migration Goal using source-of-truth, rollback, and regression-guard vocabulary. All nine checks address Markdown anatomy only; direct authority, constraint, and stop lists plus both fenced blocks have isolated failures. Alternate prose and case/spelling variants match, and document length is no longer graded.

Revision 2 gives an appropriate security-sensitive upgrade scenario with advisory, compatibility, rollback, and authority-boundary vocabulary. Its nine structural checks and 13 fixtures distinguish hierarchy, direct list shape, staged execution, verification, and report fencing without inspecting prose or length.

Revision 2 uses contemporary baseline, performance-budget, stop-condition, and final-report vocabulary while keeping factual claims outside grading. Direct-scope lists prevent nested-list substitution; every structural check has a first-failure fixture, and alternate wording remains Matched regardless of character count.

Revision 2 provides a clear release-context Goal centered on authority order, hard constraints, stop conditions, and evidence. The engine verifies only the seven-section hierarchy, direct list forms, H3 stages, and fenced verification and report blocks. All 13 fixtures pass with no prose or length operand.
